title: V2 AI-UI Component & Design Contracts
description: The design substrate for the AI-generated V2 UI. Derived from V1 packages/ui-design-system + app theme tokens (globals.css @theme, src/theme). The VISUAL layer may be regenerated, but the semantic roles, component contracts, variants, interaction contracts, content style, notification/error presentation, navigation composition, and theming extension points here are the binding behavioural source. Colour is never the sole carrier of meaning; all text is i18n-resolved; data access is BFF-only.

colour.model: Semantic CSS custom properties exposed via Tailwind v4 @theme. Runtime tenant theming overrides ONLY --color-primary; the hover shade and *-primary utilities derive from it. Strict hex allow-list (3/6/8 digit) guards against CSS injection.

safeArea.model: Capacitor SystemBars injects --safe-area-inset-*; the app maps them to --app-safe-* and pads the shell. On web, app-safe-x resolves to 0.

designTokens.focus: Focus is always visible: 2px solid var(--color-primary) outline; forced-colors keeps focus visible via system colours.

colour.danger: Destructive actions, validation/domain errors. Paired with danger-surface for backgrounds.

typography.heading-1: Page title; one per page; bound to a11y meaningful-h1.

spacing.model: Tailwind spacing scale; sections use mb-6 between blocks, gap-2/gap-3 between inline controls, p-2 for row-detail padding.

layout.app-shell: Owns the single <main> landmark, primary nav, header, and safe-area padding. Feature pages never render <main>.
layout.admin-shell: Two-pane: permission-filtered admin nav rail + content region with AdminSectionHeader.
layout.page-section: A <section> with a SectionHeader and the surface's primary content.

rules: Use onPress (not onClick) — react-aria.
destructive variant is reserved for destructive commands and pairs with a confirmation contract.
Disabled state: isDisabled + opacity-50; never remove from tab order silently.
Visible focus ring (ring-2 ring-offset-2).
Always labelled (via FormField or aria-label).
Invalid state exposes aria-invalid + linked error.
Same labelling/invalid contract as Input.

FormField.purpose: Composes Label + Input + error message + invalid state. Canonical field wrapper for RHF Controller.
rules: errorMessage is an i18n-resolved string.
isInvalid wires aria-invalid and announces the error.
items are {id,label}; label is i18n-resolved.
Inline-in-table selects require an aria-label naming the row subject.

Checkbox.note: Single-component API retained deliberately (ADR-ACT-0290/0291 — Field+Button split broke a11y).

rules: Focus trap + restore on close.
aria-label or labelled heading required.
Used for invite/confirm/destructive flows.
Tooltip is supplementary; never the sole carrier of an accessible name.
Arrow-key navigation; selected tab announced.
Default surface container; uses --radius-card / --shadow-card.
Status badges pair a colour with text — never colour alone (e.g. status label text).
Async success/failure announcements go here.
assertive for failures, polite for saved/confirmation.
Transient, non-blocking confirmations; not for errors that need recovery actions.
Standard loading presentation; message is i18n-resolved.
Recoverable error presentation; pair with a retry where applicable.
Permission-denied presentation; typically rendered by the route guard (RequirePermission).
Expandable rows via renderSubComponent.
Header cells with no visible label use sr-only text.
Row actions live in a trailing actions column.

form: RHF + zodResolver; validation bounds mirror the owning contract schema.
Submit disabled while pending; label switches to a 'saving' i18n key.
Field errors are i18n keys emitted by the Zod schema, resolved via t().
On success, forms that create reset to defaults; dialogs close.
mutation: Writes re-authorised server-side by the BFF; UI hides controls the persona lacks permission for.
On success: invalidate the relevant query key, announce via LiveRegion/toast, and refresh the contextual audit panel where one exists.
Distinguish typed failure semantics (e.g. 409 conflict) from generic failure with a specific message.
destructive: Never proceed without explicit confirmation (Dialog or typed-confirm).
Confirm button uses default/destructive emphasis; cancel uses outline.
The confirmation names the specific resource.
keyboard: All interactive controls keyboard-reachable in logical order.
Dialogs trap focus and restore it to the trigger on close.

contentStyle.voice: Clear, operator-facing, plain enterprise English. Action labels are imperative verbs (Invite, Remove, Save).
contentStyle.i18n: ALL visible text resolves through @platform/i18n-runtime t(). No string literals in UI. Interpolation via named params (e.g. {name}).
contentStyle.terminology: Use product terms consistently: member (tenant identity), tenant, entitlement, readiness, clickthrough, persona.
contentStyle.emptyVsError: Empty = a valid zero state (no data yet). Error = something failed and may be retried. Keep them distinct.

notificationPatterns.inlineLiveRegion: Per-form/section async outcome (saved / save failed). polite for success, assertive for failure.
notificationPatterns.toast: Transient global confirmations; auto-dismiss; never the only channel for a failure needing action.
notificationPatterns.alert: Persistent in-context banner for degraded/entitlement/quota/rate-limit notices.
notificationPatterns.validationSummary: Form-level error region announced to screen readers on submit failure.

errorPresentation.validationFailure: Field-level via FormField errorMessage + aria-invalid; summary announced.
errorPresentation.domainFailure: ErrorState or Alert(danger) with a specific, typed message; retry where safe. Typed conflicts (409) get a dedicated message.
errorPresentation.degraded: Alert(warning) — capability usable but limited; readiness-driven, server-truth.
errorPresentation.permissionDenied: ForbiddenState, usually at the route guard; write controls are simply absent.
errorPresentation.authRequired: Redirect to /login (route guard); never a silently-broken SPA.
errorPresentation.entitlementUnavailable: Alert(info) explaining the feature is not enabled for the tenant — not a crash, not a silent allow.
errorPresentation.quotaExceeded: Visible, with a stable reason and root-cause hint (ADR-ACT-0285 Phase 5).
errorPresentation.rateLimited: Visible, with a stable reason; non-destructive guidance to retry later.

navigationComposition.appShell: Single <main> landmark, primary navigation, header with actor display, safe-area padding.

adminRail.rule: Each item declares {to, labelKey, permission}. Items are HIDDEN when hasPermission(permission) is false (filter, not disable).
adminRail.activeState: Route-active styling; the overview item ('/admin') is exact-match.

navigationComposition.ordering: Order is the array order in ADMIN_NAV_ITEMS.

tenantBranding.endpoint: GET /api/theme (unauthenticated, tenant-scoped, always returns a usable theme)
guards: primaryColour must match strict hex allow-list (#3/6/8) or default is used.
logoUrl/faviconUrl must be http(s) URLs or are dropped.
Only --color-primary is written to the DOM at runtime; all primary shades derive from it.

themingExtensionPoints.colourScheme: Dark/prefers-color-scheme handled at :root[data-theme]; tenant override applies on top of scheme.
themingExtensionPoints.forcedColors: forced-colors/high-contrast keeps focus and meaning intact; no colour-only signalling.
